axioms: All input crossing a trust boundary is hostile until validated
A secret's validity is defined by its authoritative source, not by any cache that mirrors it
A revocation must propagate everywhere the secret was ever cached before it is complete
Confidentiality of secret material spans its whole lifetime including memory and disk at rest
failure_mode: treats 'best effort, just re-auth' at face value
ignores pure-concurrency races with no security angle
